content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Telephone • • Registration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662         322-5660         322-5901         322-6441         322-6444

March 2, 1984


Nancy C. Miller, Esq.
Law Offices of Richard H. Hyde, Inc.
111 Capitol Mall Building, Suite 225
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-84-017

Dear Nancy:

     This letter is sent to respond to your request for advice
dated January 30, 1984, concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act and to confirm our
telephone conversation of February 28, 1984.

     You asked whether, in reporting expenditures made to a
partnership operating under a fictitious business name
statement, a committee may list the partnership as payee or
whether it is required to also list as payees the individual
partners.  I advised you that only the name of the actual payee,
i.e. the partnership in this example, is required.

     If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-6444.

                                   Sincerely,


                                   Diane Maura Fishburn
                                   Staff Counsel
                                   Legal Division

DMF:pln

FPPC
FEB 2 8 14 AM '84

LAW OFFICES
OF
RICHARD H. HYDE, INC.
111 Capitol Mall Building, Suite 225
Sacramento, California 95814
Telephone: (916) 447-7933

January 30, 1984

Carla Wardlow
FPPC
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Carla:

I am requesting an opinion as to reporting requirements for campaign committees. Specifically when a campaign committee is reporting an expense or payment made to a Partnership operating under a ficitious business name statement, does the committee list the partnership name only, or is it required to list individual partners as well?

If a regulation exists as to this question, please send me a copy of it. If you need additional information, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Very truly yours,

HYDE, MILLER and SAVAGE

By: [signature]
NANCY C. MILLER

NCM:so

sections.question: You asked whether, in reporting expenditures made to a partnership operating under a fictitious business name statement, a committee may list the partnership as payee or whether it is required to also list as payees the individual partners.
sections.conclusion: I advised you that only the name of the actual payee, i.e. the partnership in this example, is required.
sections.question_synthetic: When a campaign committee reports an expense paid to a partnership operating under a fictitious business name, must the committee list only the partnership name or must it also identify the individual partners?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Under campaign disclosure provisions, a committee need only list the actual payee (the partnership) and is not required to also identify individual partners.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (dated January 30, 1984) and FPPC response (dated March 2, 1984). The response letter is the primary document. Clear question and conclusion sections present in FPPC response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked whether, in reporting expenditures made to a partnership operating under a fictitious business name statement, a committee may list the partnership as payee or whether it is required to also list as payees the individual partners.

CONCLUSION: I advised you that only the name of the actual payee, i.e. the partnership in this example, is required.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to a January 30, 1984 request regarding campaign disclosure reporting requirements when expenditures are made to partnerships operating under fictitious business names. The FPPC advised that only the partnership name as the actual payee is required in the report.
